picks.reasoning: Spain sit comfortably within my target odds range at 1.97 and represent the stronger footballing unit at this stage of the tournament. Their recent international pedigree, superior squad depth, and tactical cohesion under De la Fuente make them the most statistically sound selection across all three matches today. Portugal can be dangerous, but Spain's consistency at major tournaments gives them a clear edge here.

England at 2.52 is decent value against a Mexico side that's going to be absolutely pumped on home soil energy — but at a World Cup knockout stage, England's squad depth and tournament experience has to count for something. Mexico are dangerous but England have the firepower to see this out. Greggs pulling the Mexican sarnie is basically a sign from the universe.

Portugal at 4.3 on home soil in a World Cup knockout scenario? Yes please! Ronaldo's legacy, Félix's flair, and the burning desire to finally knock Spain off their high horse — this is a team playing for national pride and that means dangerous. Spain might be the bookies' darling but Iberian derbies are NEVER comfortable, and I'll take 4.3 all day long for the upset of the tournament.
